name: South Dakota Speech-Language Pathologist License
description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to South Dakota - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: Board of Hearing Aid Dispensers and Audiologists

address.description:
South Dakota Board of Examiners for Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology
810 N. Main St., Suite 203
Spearfish, SD 57783

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a South Dakota SLP license cost?
Is South Dakota part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?
Does South Dakota allow telepractice for SLPs?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, with a valid SD license.

mainEntity.name: Does South Dakota have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. South Dakota participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in South Dakota without a separate endorsement application.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to South Dakota?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in South Dakota while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
